mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55 percent of observations during sustained FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.5 percent indicates labor market deterioration that triggers earnings downgrade expectations and risk-off portfolio flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production signal a coordinated demand-supply contraction that amplifies recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to predict negative S&P 500 20-day returns with Rank IC below 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession housing recovery, month-over-month increases in housing starts predict construction and consumer durable demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases of greater than 5 percent over 3 months coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than half of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding corporate operating leverage and revenue beats, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment alongside rising retail sales indicates a broad-based demand and income expansion that reduces recession probability and lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint increases in PAYEMS and RSAFS are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of joint-up months.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index acceleration above 4.0 percent year-over-year forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, raising real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 4.0 percent, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive more than 50 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month industrial production growth signals improving factory utilization and margin expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive changes in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations.
